steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The "but in fact" indicates these sculptures contradict the characterization of Greco-Roman art as idealizing. We need a word meaning the opposite — something that describes portraying people in a negative rather than perfect way.

steps.title: Match the opposite to the choices
steps.content:
The blank must mean "the opposite of idealizing" — depicting people unfavorably, not beautifully. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — superficial: shallow or lacking depth — a superficial depiction doesn't directly oppose idealization; it describes quality of observation, not attractiveness. ✗
  • Choice B — implausible: unbelievable or unrealistic — this targets believability, not the flattering-vs-unflattering contrast the passage sets up. ✗
  • Choice C — unflattering: presenting someone in a negative or unappealing way — directly opposes idealization, which portrays subjects beautifully. ✓
  • Choice D — imprecise: inaccurate or vague — this targets accuracy, not the aesthetic praise vs. criticism contrast signaled by "idealizing." ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
